intro: Elleanthus lepidus, also known as the hairy Elleanthus, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae) native to Southeast Asia. Elleanthus lepidus has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. The bioactive constituents of Elleanthus lepidus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elleanthus lepidus is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
